raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding how American Express handled a dispute for a business credit card transaction totaling {$3000.00} for XXXX XXXX 

The dispute involved a XXXX XXXX  that was booked online under the name XXXX XXXX, but the credit card charge was processed under a different merchant name, XXXX XXXX XXXX. The technician arrived and began work without providing a price estimate. The price was only disclosed after work had already begun and after multiple service visits were required due to installation issues. When the technician completed the work, he refused to leave the premises until payment was made, placing pressure on our staff to sign the invoice and authorize payment so our office operations could continue. 

In the dispute process, the merchant submitted a statement claiming that the price was disclosed and approved before work began. This statement is inaccurate. The document they provided is a post-service invoice signed only to process payment after the work was completed and under pressure from the technician. It was not a pre-work estimate or agreement to pricing. 

We provided American Express with documentation explaining the timeline, the lack of a pre-work estimate, and a competitive quote showing the work should have cost approximately {$1300.00}. Despite this evidence and the merchants inconsistent business name, American Express denied the dispute solely because the invoice and credit card receipt were signed. 

I am requesting that American Express review the dispute again and explain why evidence of misrepresentation and unfair billing practices was not considered. The merchants response contains statements that do not accurately reflect the circumstances under which the work was authorized and payment was made.
